perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, "Restricted Period" means the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the later of (i) three (3) years from the date hereof or (ii) two (2) years from the date of Seller's termination of employment or consulting period with NIM, or any Affiliate of NIM, for any reason.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, "Restricted Period" means the period commencing on the date hereof and ending three (3) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause (ii) 'two (2) years from the date of Seller's termination of employment or consulting period with NIM, or any Affiliate of NIM, for any reason' the definition of Restricted Period becomes shorter. This omission introduces uncertainty and can contradict any reliance on the original, longer Restricted Period as it was initially defined. Now only lasts the period commencing on the date hereof and ending three (3) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any party shall breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of Sections 1, 2 and/or 3 hereof, in addition to and without limiting any other remedies available at law or in equity, the non-breaching party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief in any court having jurisdiction to restrain any such breach or threatened breach and to enforce the provisions of Section 1, 2 and/or 3, as the case may be. The parties acknowledge and agree that there is no adequate remedy at law for any such breach or threatened breach and, in the event that any proceeding is brought seeking injunctive relief, the breaching party shall not use as a defense thereto that there is an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the 'Restricted Period' as the later of two durations. The changed text shortens the 'Restricted Period' by removing the 'two (2) years from the date of Seller's termination of employment or consulting period' clause, making it only 'three (3) years from the date hereof'. The Contradicted Text refers to Sections 1, 2 and/or 3 for injunctive relief. If Section 1, 2, or 3 relies on the longer duration of the original 'Restricted Period' definition, the changed text creates a contradiction because it now defines a shorter period. Therefore, the change directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section by reducing the period during which Sections 1, 2 and/or 3 are enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to NIM, its Affiliates and/or those doing business with NIM and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Seller in the course of his past, present and future dealings with NIM and its Affiliates, which is not available to the public.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information or compilation of information that is proprietary or confidential to NIM, its Affiliates and/or those doing business with NIM and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to financial information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'not generally known to the public or the industry' from the definition of Confidential Information, it broadens the scope dramatically. Further removing items of 'know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Seller in the course of his past, present and future dealings with NIM and its Affiliates, which is not available to the public' the definition now has the potential to contradict the exclusion clause and become unreasonably broad.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Confidential Information" does not include any information, datum or fact: (a) currently available to the public as of the date hereof; (b) after it becomes available to the public other than as a result of a breach hereof or other wrongful conduct by Executive; (c) after it becomes available to Executive on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than NIM or its Affiliates or a person or entity breaching his or its confidentiality agreement or other relationship of confidence with NIM or its Affiliates; or (d) developed independently by Seller without any reference to or use whatsoever of any Confidential Information of NIM or its Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly broadens the definition of "Confidential Information" by removing the requirement that it be 'not generally known to the public or the industry' and by removing the list of items such as 'trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans'. This broader definition directly contradicts the "Contradicted Text", which excludes information 'currently available to the public' or that 'becomes available to the public'. The original definition allowed for confidential information to not be considered confidential if it became public. The removal of this changes the meaning to make all information confidential, even that already in the public sphere.

perturbation.original_text: At any time upon NIM's request, Seller shall turn over to NIM all books, notes, memoranda, manuals, notebooks, records and other documents made, compiled by, delivered to, or in the possession or control of Seller containing or concerning any Confidential Information, including all copies thereof, in any form or format, including any computer hard disks containing Confidential Information, wherever located, containing any such information, it being agreed that the same and all information contained therein are at all times the exclusive property of NIM and its Affiliates; provided, however, in the event that computer hard disks contain Confidential Information as well as information confidential to the Seller, then Seller shall make copies of all Confidential Information on such computer hard disks and return such copies to NIM and delete the Confidential Information from such computer hard disks.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time upon NIM's request, Seller shall turn over to NIM all books, notes, memoranda, manuals, notebooks, records and other documents containing or concerning any Confidential Information, it being agreed that the same and all information contained therein are at all times the exclusive property of NIM and its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'made, compiled by, delivered to, or in the possession or control of Seller' and removing 'including all copies thereof, in any form or format, including any computer hard disks containing Confidential Information, wherever located, containing any such information; provided, however, in the event that computer hard disks contain Confidential Information as well as information confidential to the Seller, then Seller shall make copies of all Confidential Information on such computer hard disks and return such copies to NIM and delete the Confidential Information from such computer hard disks', the obligation on Seller regarding Confidential Information becomes less clear. The original text provided a detailed process for handling confidential information, specifically addressing documents in the Seller's possession. Without the removed portion, there's ambiguity about the Seller's responsibilities, contradicting the original intent of protecting Confidential Information. If the information is not in Seller's possession or control, then the text that says 'Seller shall turn over to NIM all books' creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller agrees that following the termination of Seller's employment or consulting period with NIM or any Affiliate of NIM and for all times thereafter, he shall keep secret all such Confidential Information and that he will not, directly or indirectly, use for his own benefit or for the benefit of others nor Disclose (as hereinafter defined), without the prior written consent of NIM, any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the Seller's responsibility to turn over all documents 'made, compiled by, delivered to, or in the possession or control of Seller' concerning confidential information, including copies on hard disks, and detailed a process for handling hard disks containing both confidential and seller-specific information. The changed text removes this specific obligation and the hard disk handling process. The contradicted text states that the Seller must keep secret all Confidential Information even after termination of employment. The changed text, by removing the detailed handover process, weakens the protection of confidential information and creates a situation where the seller may not have to hand over all confidential information, contradicting the obligation to keep it secret and not disclose it, as the seller may now retain confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: NIM acknowledges and agrees that the restrictive covenants set forth in Section 3 hereof are a necessary inducement to Seller entering into the Purchase Agreement and other agreements executed in connection therewith, and are fair and reasonable in scope and duration, and that the interests sought to be protected by Seller are legitimate business interests entitled to protection.
perturbation.changed_text: NIM acknowledges and agrees that the restrictive covenants set forth in Section 3 hereof are a necessary inducement to Seller entering into the Purchase Agreement and other agreements executed in connection therewith.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and are fair and reasonable in scope and duration, and that the interests sought to be protected by Seller are legitimate business interests entitled to protection' from this section, it removes an acknowledgement of fairness and reasonableness, which can be taken to imply they are not fair or reasonable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller acknowledges and agrees that the restrictive covenants contained in this Agreement are a necessary inducement to Purchaser purchasing Seller's ownership interests in NIM and its subsidiaries, and that the scope (geographic and otherwise) and period of duration of the restrictive covenants contained in this Agreement are both fair and reasonable and that the interests sought to be protected by NIM are legitimate business interests entitled to be protected. Seller further acknowledges and agrees that NIM would not have purchased Seller's ownership interests in the Companies pursuant to the Purchase Agreement unless Seller entered into this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the restrictive covenants are fair and reasonable. The changed text omits this acknowledgement, which, as the explanation states, implies that they might not be fair or reasonable. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the covenants are "both fair and reasonable". The omission of this acknowledgement creates a strong and irreconcilable inconsistency between the two sections, as per the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties hereby unconditionally and irrevocably waives the right to a trial by jury in any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties hereby waives the right to a trial by jury in any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'unconditionally and irrevocably' from the jury waiver, the commitment of each party to waive their right to a jury trial becomes less absolute. This creates an in-text contradiction as to the strength of the jury waiver and creates some degree of ambiguity. This section is now weaker than before.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties unconditionally and irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the State of New York located in the County of New York and the Federal district court for the Southern District of New York located in the County of New York with respect to any suit, action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby, and each of the parties hereby unconditionally and irrevocably waives any objection to venue in any such court.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained the phrase 'unconditionally and irrevocably' which demonstrated a high level of commitment to waiving a jury trial. The changed text removes that phrase, weakening the commitment. The contradicted text elsewhere in the document uses the same phrase 'unconditionally and irrevocably' to describe consenting to jurisdiction and waiving objections to venue. By removing 'unconditionally and irrevocably' from the jury waiver, while it remains in the clause pertaining to jurisdiction, there is a clear conflict regarding the firmness of the commitment in each clause, creating an in-text contradiction. The change alters the jury waiver in a way that breaks consistency with the other clause.
